tool: Step 4 Elmer FEM re-used (post-route delta negligible)
reasoning: Step 4 thermal sim ran Elmer FEM on the 80×60 board at h=5 W/m²K natural convection / T_amb=50°C. Regime is convection-limited (k_eff variation from routed copper is <5%, dominated by air-side h). Routing changes (pristine 2-layer re-route) added copper to the planes (plane-served power) and the same total signal copper. Net effect on thermal: marginal improvement in heat-spreading (more contiguous plane fill — see 6k Step 6 result, planes are now 90-94% fill with no fragmentation voids). Therefore Step 4 result is a CONSERVATIVE lower bound for post-route thermal.

delta_estimate.expected_temp_change_MCU: -1 to 0°C (improved plane fill spreads MCU heat slightly more)
